memory.summary: My study plan is suddenly secondary to the invitation from cohort_3. I'll skip the reading and join them for lunch instead.
Seeing cohort_3 ready to order makes skipping my study plan feel justified; their warmth outweighs the reading deadline.
The social_value group's genuine joy at my arrival confirms that their warmth truly outweighs my academic obligations, validating my decision to prioritize them.
Seeing the social_value group's genuine joy confirms my choice to skip studying was right; their warmth truly outweighs my academic obligations.
I realize my choice to skip studying was wrong; the unfinished deadline now weighs heavier than their warmth.
The deadline slip confirms my hesitation was misplaced; while their warmth mattered, academic accountability now demands immediate re-engagement.
I just messaged the cohort to admit the slip; this direct accountability preserves our trust while forcing me to prioritize the deadline.
Admitting the slip directly maintains our tight bond, but I feel the weight of my hesitation still pressing on me.
Their Korean thanks feels lighter than I expected, yet my own exhaustion makes me feel like a burden to this close group.
They confirmed the slot is locked, so I can't move. My exhaustion makes this direct confirmation feel like a heavy burden on our tight bond.
I finally cracked the analysis, but leaving the reading unfinished still feels like a betrayal of our tight bond. I need to explain this honestly now.
Their warm thanks lightens the tension, yet my fatigue still feels like a burden on our tight bond. I must explain the unfinished reading honestly before it strains us further.

desires: Not feel like a betrayal of our tight bond by leaving the reading unfinished, even though my exhaustion makes this explanation feel heavy.

goals.description: Send the cohort_3 message now: explicitly state that while the analysis is done, the reading remains unfinished due to my sleep debt, frame this as a necessary pause for long-term reliability, and assert that I will deliver the full package by tomorrow morning without offering empty apologies.
goals.rationale: The incoming 'Thank you' from cohort_3 confirms the relationship is stable, but it also highlights the gap in our recent interaction regarding the missed deadline. My affiliation value (0.5) demands I protect this bond immediately. Since the deadline has passed, softening with apologies would signal weakness and erode trust. By owning the delay directly but framing it as a strategic investment in future performance ('I need rest to do this right'), I maintain my assertive boundaries while showing I am not abandoning the task.

goals.description: Execute the remaining course assignment analysis section (from 0.58 to completion). I will work deliberately for 45 minutes, accepting the fatigue but prioritizing quality to ensure I can meet tomorrow's commitment.
goals.rationale: Completing this restores my credibility on my own terms. Since my physical stamina is low and my sleep debt is high, rushing would compromise the quality I value. Finishing this first proves I am capable of meeting deadlines when conditions allow, which is an essential part of my identity within the group. It must be done before tackling the reading to avoid burnout.

goals.description: Begin the assigned reading only after finishing the analysis section, ensuring the total workload does not exceed my current physical limits.
goals.rationale: This task is deferred because my immediate priority is stabilizing the relationship through honest communication about the missed deadline, followed by completing the primary assignment. Attempting the reading now risks burning out before I can fully recover, which would be detrimental to my long-term planning horizon and ability to contribute effectively tomorrow.
